priority_fixes.issue: Entanglement hypothesis presented as named contribution without empirical support beyond H3 Spearman r=+0.35. 'Hub feature' analogy introduced without any network analysis.
priority_fixes.action: Remove named hypothesis or label as speculative interpretation. Add empirical support (activation frequency correlation, decoder centrality analysis) or explicitly label as hypothesis requiring validation.

priority_fixes.issue: CMI dimension instability: correlation sign reverses at d'>=20 (d'=10: rho=-0.383; d'=20: rho=+0.048; d'=30: rho=+0.299). Bonferroni p=0.236. Cherry-picking d'=10 is unjustified.
priority_fixes.action: Either pre-register d'=10 as primary dimension with justification, or analyze all dimensions with multiple comparison correction. Add ablation and baseline comparisons.

priority_fixes.issue: Probe quality confound: absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). Low-CMI letters may be harder to probe, inflating absorption estimates. No partial correlation computed.
priority_fixes.action: Compute partial correlation controlling for probe F1. Restrict CMI analysis to quality-gated letters (F1>0.85). Acknowledge as limitation.

priority_fixes.issue: OrtSAE ablation compares OrtSAE-without-penalty (L0~920) to OrtSAE-with-penalty (L0~550) to conclude penalty effectiveness. This is comparing at UNMATCHED L0—the very confound the paper criticizes.
priority_fixes.action: Compare OrtSAE variants at matched L0 values, or compare each to vanilla SAE at similar L0. Remove self-contradictory ablation conclusion.

high_priority_fixes.issue: r~+0.93 sparsity-absorption correlation based on n=4 with TWO points at identical L0=50. Only 3 unique L0 values. Correlation with 2 df is mathematically fragile.
high_priority_fixes.action: Add more data points or explicitly acknowledge limitation. Present as descriptive rather than inferential.

high_priority_fixes.issue: MCC ~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control suggests chance-level recovery. Paper acknowledges MCC failure but does not address implication: absorption reduction may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical recovery.
high_priority_fixes.action: Discuss what MCC=0.21 means for validity of all absorption metrics. Address whether absorption reduction is meaningful given chance-level MCC.

high_priority_fixes.issue: Section 3.5 promises ANOVA across all variants but this is not reported. With 3 variants and 5 replicates (2,12 df), power is low. Unfulfilled promise undermines credibility.
high_priority_fixes.action: Either report the ANOVA or remove the promise from Section 3.5. Explain transparently if skipped due to low power.

high_priority_fixes.issue: Abstract overclaims: 'causally important features' rather than 'highly steerable.' Steering sensitivity measures manipulability, not causal importance per se.
high_priority_fixes.action: Replace 'causally important' with 'highly steerable' or 'computationally central' in abstract and throughout paper.

medium_priority_fixes.issue: L0 confound as 'contribution' overstates novelty. SAEBench (Karvonen et al., 2025) already notes sparsity differences across architectures.
medium_priority_fixes.action: Reframe L0 confound as contribution to experimental methodology, not standalone research contribution.

medium_priority_fixes.issue: Effect size inconsistency: Section 4.2 says '~15%' but correct calculation is 18.4%.
medium_priority_fixes.action: Use '18%' consistently throughout (as abstract correctly states).

medium_priority_fixes.issue: Null controls at alpha=5 show no absorption effect (p=0.94) while main experiment shows 18% difference. Post-hoc explanation of alpha-dependence not pre-registered.
medium_priority_fixes.action: Run null controls at alpha=10 and alpha=20 to confirm alpha-dependence, or pre-register the explanation.

medium_priority_fixes.issue: Chanin absorption score referenced but undefined in paper body.
medium_priority_fixes.action: Add one sentence defining the Chanin absorption score (first-letter probe method) before Section 4.4.

continue_from_previous.issue: [From previous critique] H2 pilot numbers (70.9% reduction) contradict full-scale results (4.5x increase). Pilot and full H2 used different absorption metrics.
continue_from_previous.action: Audit all H2 results. Remove pilot H2 numbers or clearly label as pilot-only. Do not present as consistent.

continue_from_previous.issue: [From previous critique] H5 'directional confirmation' uses non-existent 5% threshold. Actual criterion was >8%; achieved 2.51%.
continue_from_previous.action: Change H5 framing to FAILED: causal task delta 2.51% vs required >8%. Remove fabricated 5% threshold.

continue_from_previous.issue: [From previous critique] Absorption metric inconsistency between pilot (first-letter probe, ~0.225 vanilla) and full-scale (Gini, ~0.015 vanilla).
continue_from_previous.action: Standardize on one absorption metric. Document which metric is used where.
